title: izba dogfood journeys (Phase 1 output / Phase 2 input)
description: A feature's user journeys: goals a real user would have given the spec's promises, decomposed into ordered intent+expectation steps. Produced by the local Phase 1 (intent extraction) harness; consumed by the Phase 2 (journey execution) runner.

feature.description: Human-readable name of the feature under test.

journey_id.description: Stable, unique-within-feature kebab-case id. Used for sharding and loop-dedup.

rationale.description: Why this journey exists: the spec promise / expectation it probes.

tier.description: Progressive-exploration depth (Phase 2 sequencing). 'smoke' = shallow happy-path + capability probes run first and cheap; 'core' = main feature coverage; 'deep' = adversarial / edge / multi-step. Deeper tiers are dispatched only after the prior tier's gating journeys established the prerequisite capabilities (post in-place fix). Optional; absent ⇒ treated as 'core'.

establishes.description: Capability ids this journey proves usable when it genuinely succeeds (e.g. 'install-tooling-under-enforce', 'tls-verifies-under-enforce'). Smoke journeys establish the prerequisites deeper journeys depend on. Optional; kebab-case capability tokens shared across journeys of one feature.

requires.description: Capability ids that must be established (by an earlier-tier journey, possibly after an in-place fix) before this journey is worth running. If a required capability is a confirmed unfixable blocker, the sequencer/orchestrator defers this journey and records why (never silently). Optional.

gating.description: When true, this journey is a gate for its tier: advancing to the next tier requires it to genuinely succeed (or its blocker to be fixed in-place). Use sparingly — only for the capability prerequisites deeper tiers truly depend on. Optional; default false.

kind.description: Where the expectation was traced from (spec | pr | greptile review | --help topic | README).

ref.description: Pointer into the source (doc + section, PR number, review id, help topic).

intent.description: What the user wants to do at this step (intent, NOT a literal command). The Actor chooses the concrete izba invocation.

expect.description: The expected observable outcome of this step, in user-observable terms (judged by the rubric judge against product state — NOT a literal exit-code assertion).
